About Clifford Chigbu

Clifford Chigbu is a California attorney and your dedicated legal advocate in Elk Grove. He's built his solo practice around one idea: families going through hard times deserve a lawyer who answers the phone, tells them the truth, and treats their case like it matters — because it does.

He doesn't fill the office with photos of himself or walls of diplomas. He fills it with clients who walk in worried and walk out with a plan. Whether you're navigating divorce, custody, or any of life's unexpected legal challenges, you'll get straight answers and experienced representation.

If a case isn't a fit, he'll tell you. If it is, he'll fight for it like it's his own.

Tell us about your case

The fastest way to get help. Tell us briefly what's going on — we'll review and reach out to set up a consultation. Personal injury consultations are free; other matters carry a modest consultation fee that's credited toward your case if you retain us.

✓ Received. We'll be in touch shortly.

Practice Areas

Want to give us more detail up front? Each practice area below has a longer intake form. Filling it out helps Mr. Chigbu give you a real assessment faster — and the deeper you go, the more useful the consultation will be.

Family-based petitions, green cards, naturalization, deportation defense, work visas.

Bankruptcy

Chapter 7 and Chapter 13. Stop garnishments, foreclosure, harassment from creditors.

Start confidential intake →

Business & Contract Law

Contract drafting and review, business formation, disputes, and negotiation.

Start confidential intake →

Ask a question — instant answers

Type any legal question below. The answers come straight from Clifford's years of practice.

How long does a divorce take in California?

Six months minimum from the date the other party is served. Most cases settle before trial. Complex or contested matters can take a year or more.

How is child custody decided in California?

California courts decide custody based on the best interests of the child. Courts prefer arrangements that let both parents stay involved, unless there's evidence one parent would harm the child.

How is child support calculated?

California uses a statewide formula based on both parents' incomes, the amount of time each parent has the child, and certain deductions. The result is what's called 'guideline' support.

Do I need grounds to get divorced in California?

No. California is a no-fault divorce state — 'irreconcilable differences' is the only grounds you need.

What is a restraining order and how do I get one?

A domestic violence restraining order protects you from abuse, harassment, or stalking. You file a request, the court can issue a temporary order right away, and there's a hearing within 21 days.

Can I modify child support or custody later?

Yes — if there's been a significant change in circumstances (income, move, work schedule, etc.), either parent can ask the court to modify the order.

Do both spouses need to agree to divorce?

No. One spouse can file and proceed even if the other objects. The court will still grant the divorce.

How is property divided in a California divorce?

California is a community property state. Assets and debts acquired during the marriage are generally split 50/50. Assets owned before marriage or received by gift/inheritance stay separate.

Can I get spousal support (alimony)?

Possibly. California courts consider the length of the marriage, each spouse's earning capacity, standard of living during the marriage, and several other factors.

What is the difference between legal and physical custody?

Legal custody is the right to make decisions (school, medical, religion). Physical custody is where the child lives. Either can be joint or sole.

Can I apply for a green card through marriage?

Yes, if you're married to a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident. The process involves a petition (I-130) and an adjustment of status or consular processing, depending on where you are.

How long does it take to get a green card?

It varies dramatically — from months (for immediate relatives of U.S. citizens) to many years (for certain family-based or employment categories with long backlogs).

Can I sponsor my parents for a green card?

Yes, if you're a U.S. citizen over 21. Parents of U.S. citizens are classified as immediate relatives and there is no annual cap.

What is DACA and do I qualify?

Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals provides protection and work authorization for certain people brought to the U.S. as children. Eligibility is based on arrival age, continuous residence, and other factors.

I'm in deportation proceedings — what do I do?

Contact an immigration attorney immediately. There are several forms of relief that may apply, but deadlines are strict and missing one can be catastrophic. Call our office right away.

How do I become a U.S. citizen?

Most green card holders can apply for naturalization after 5 years (3 if married to a U.S. citizen). You'll file Form N-400, attend a biometrics appointment, pass an English and civics test, and take an oath.

Can I file my own immigration paperwork?

Yes — but immigration forms are notoriously unforgiving. A small mistake can cause years of delay or a denial. An attorney review is usually worth it.

What if I have a criminal record?

Even minor offenses can cause serious immigration consequences. Do not rely on general advice — consult a lawyer who knows both criminal and immigration law before any filing.

Can I work while my green card is pending?

Usually yes, by filing a separate application (Form I-765) for employment authorization. The EAD card is typically issued within several months.

What is asylum?

Asylum is protection for people who've been persecuted or fear persecution in their home country based on race, religion, nationality, political opinion, or membership in a particular social group. Generally you must apply within one year of arrival.

How much is my personal injury case worth?

Depends on the severity of injuries, medical bills, lost wages, pain and suffering, and liability. We give honest estimates after reviewing your case — never inflated promises.

How long do I have to file a personal injury claim in California?

Generally 2 years from the date of injury for most cases, but it can be much shorter (as little as 6 months) for claims against government entities. Don't wait — call.

Should I give a statement to the insurance company?

No. Anything you say can be used to reduce or deny your claim. Talk to us before giving any statement — including your own insurance company.

What if I was partly at fault?

California uses 'pure comparative negligence' — even if you were partially at fault, you can still recover damages, reduced by your percentage of fault.

How much does a personal injury lawyer cost?

We work on contingency — no fees up front. We only get paid if you recover money, and our fee is a percentage of the recovery.

Should I see a doctor after an accident even if I feel fine?

Yes. Some injuries don't show symptoms for days. A prompt medical record also protects your claim — insurance companies argue that gaps in treatment mean you weren't really hurt.

What if the other driver was uninsured?

Your own policy may have uninsured motorist coverage. We can file a claim against your own insurance, and there may be other responsible parties to pursue.

How long does a personal injury case take?

Simple cases can settle in months. Complex cases or those that go to trial can take 1-2 years. We'll give you a realistic timeline after reviewing your case.

Will I lose my house if I file bankruptcy?

Usually no. California's homestead exemption protects a significant amount of home equity for primary residences. We'll review your specific situation.

What's the difference between Chapter 7 and Chapter 13?

Chapter 7 wipes out qualifying debts in a few months but has income limits. Chapter 13 is a 3-5 year repayment plan that lets you catch up on a mortgage or car payment while keeping the asset.

Will bankruptcy stop wage garnishment?

Yes — the automatic stay stops most garnishments the moment we file your case, usually within 24 hours.

How bad does bankruptcy hurt my credit?

Bankruptcy stays on your credit report for 7-10 years, but most clients see their credit score recover faster than expected because they're starting with zero debt.

Can I file bankruptcy more than once?

Yes, but there are time limits. Generally 8 years between Chapter 7 filings, and 2-6 years in other combinations. We'll walk you through your options.

What debts can bankruptcy NOT wipe out?

Most student loans, recent taxes, child support, alimony, and debts from fraud or DUI-related injuries are generally not dischargeable. We'll tell you what applies to your situation.

Do I qualify for Chapter 7?

Chapter 7 has an income means test. If your household income is below the California median for your family size, you automatically qualify. Above that, we do a more detailed analysis.

Will my employer find out if I file bankruptcy?

Usually not, unless you're in a Chapter 13 with wage deductions, or your employer is a creditor. Bankruptcy is public record but nobody is notified automatically.

Do I need a lawyer to form an LLC?

Not legally — but a DIY filing often misses operating agreement details that matter when disputes come up later. A small upfront investment saves big headaches.

Should I have a lawyer review a contract before I sign?

For anything significant, yes. We offer flat-rate contract reviews and can usually turn around a contract in a few days.

What do I do if someone breached our contract?

Call us before you send any demand letters or talk to the other side. What you say early can affect the remedies you have later.

LLC or corporation — which should I choose?

Depends on taxes, ownership structure, liability, and long-term plans. LLCs are more flexible; corporations are better for certain kinds of investment and growth. We'll walk you through it.

What goes in a partnership agreement?

Ownership percentages, responsibilities, profit distribution, decision-making authority, dispute resolution, and exit terms. A well-drafted partnership agreement prevents most partnership disputes.

How much does a consultation cost?

Personal injury consultations are free. For other matters, we charge a modest consultation fee that's credited toward your case if you retain us.

Where is your office?

4815 Laguna Park Dr, Suite C, Elk Grove, CA 95758. We also do phone and video consultations.

What are your hours?

Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM. After-hours by appointment.

Do you speak any languages besides English?

Yes. We can also arrange interpreters for Spanish and other languages on request.

How quickly can I meet with an attorney?

Same day or next day in most cases. Call (916) 230-6381.

Do you take payment plans?

For most matters, yes. Tell us your situation honestly and we'll work out something reasonable.

Is my conversation with you confidential?

Yes. Attorney-client confidentiality protects everything you tell us from the moment you reach out — even if you don't ultimately hire us.

What areas do you serve?

Elk Grove, Sacramento, Davis, Lodi, Stockton, Jackson, Galt, Wilton, Rancho Cordova, Folsom, Ione, West Sacramento, and the greater Central Valley.

Proudly serving the greater Sacramento area

Clifford serves clients in Elk Grove and across the surrounding communities.

Common questions

Is the consultation free?

Personal injury consultations are free — and PI cases are handled on contingency, meaning no fee unless we win. For other matters, we charge a modest consultation fee that's credited toward your case if you decide to retain us.

Do you take cases on a payment plan?

For most matters, yes. We'll work out something that fits your situation in the first meeting.

What languages do you speak?

English and Igbo. We can also arrange interpreters for Spanish and other languages on request.

Where is your office?

Elk Grove, California — serving the greater Sacramento area. We meet by appointment and also do phone and video consultations.

How fast can we meet?

Same day or next day in most cases. Call the number on this page.

Are you really a licensed attorney?

Yes — Mr. Chigbu is an active member in good standing of the State Bar of California. You can verify any time at calbar.ca.gov.
